title: Underwater music flag not reset when going back to main menu sometimes?
author: jukibom
body:
Reported @ https://steamcommunity.com/app/1781750/discussions/0/797838226728437380/

Not entirely sure it's the underwater trigger, but there's a couple things that sets this effect (e.g. pause menu). Should be fairly easy to add a reset to the main menu in any case

title: Non conforming espect ratios for level thumbnails cause trouble + author credit

title: A folder for custom races
author: CardamomCake
body:
I mean a folder in which custom maps can be stored, so they can show up in map selection, under a new custom tag or something. 

This is something I have wanted to tackle for a while, and I think it is something that the game could use, but there are a couple of implementation questions that have kept me from starting on it

* Firstly where should the folder be located?, under /documents/mygames/flydangerous or something?, or should it be in the game folder where the rest of the game data is stored. (No opinion, first is better for a by hand approach of file-sharing, second is better for a integrated approach, I assume)

* Should a active index be stored in a file somewhere, or should the index of custom races be built from scanning the folder, each time the index is first called. (I lean towards the second approach)

* Should the game expect a folder structure similar to how races are saved in the code-base (thumbnail's and stuff), or should it be a folder in which the json files are dumped. (I think the first approach is better here)

I don't think a GUI/server implementation for downloading/saving custom level needs to be solved/considered for this issue yet, since it should not be difficult to integrate that solution in with the folder.

title: Competitive mode(s) in multiplayer
author: topas-rec
body:
I host a few LAN party's throughout the year and would like to try out competitive racing with friends (hosted server - which is already implemented).

The competitive mode(s) is currently not implemented.

Running a multiplayer server works in free-roam mode.

_I didn't find an existing issue for this, so I created one - correct this if I'm wrong._

title: Targeted velocity multiplier.
author: CardamomCake
body:
If uncapped velocity builds are going to be part of the game, not necessary as a default config, but as something that is supported in general. I do think there is one mayor issue that needs to be resolved.

This issue is the vector assist problem. It tries to map the ship to a speed, but since the speed is unreasonably high, it will accelerate fa on users to uncontrollable speeds. The obvious option is adding a flight assist targeted speed modifier, that brings that speed down to more manageable levels. Maybe a bit against the spirit of managing it yourself, but it probably is more important that fa-on players also have a good experience. 

The only problem is that if this modifier is handled as a flight parameter, It could be (ab)used as a fa-off check by mapmakers. Maybe good for maps that are expressly for teaching fa-off for example, maybe toxic if used differently. I think you should have final say in how that is handled. 

I'm willing to be the one writing the code for this one of course, should be relatively easy, and I know this part of the code.

title: Multiple tiers of "official" ship configs
author: jukibom
body:
I'd like to expand the current leader board with some additional tiers, to both make the game a little more beginner-friendly and also expand the skill ceiling with a ridiculous config.

The configs should work with existing maps, I don't have the capacity to build config-specific maps unless we REALLY have to.

- [ ] Beginner config
- [ ] Expert config
- [ ] (stretch) anything-goes-within-reason config
- [ ] UI to support selections (per-level? as part of the time trial menu? on the leaderboard? something else?)

others?

title: Hiding mass and inetriaTensorMultiplier from ship config settings, and possibly adding boostReverseEffectiveness.
author: CardamomCake
body:
Mass and inetriaTensorMultiplier are redundant in the flight model outside collisions, so hiding them as editable in the settings might be a good idea in order to make different configs more easily comparable to each other. 

boostReverseEffectiveness would influence how much you can counteract the forced forward thrust from boosting, the default now is a hard-coded 40% if I remember correctly. 

I think adding the option for boostReverseEffectiveness makes sense because the there are many options for tweaking almost every aspect of how the boost works. This is like the only mayor part of the flight model that has no option to tweak it that I can think of. 

Thoughts?

P.S. I am willing to try to do this issue myself, but in that case I could use some directions to the files which would need tweaking.

title: Add ability to disable the alternate flight controls toggle sfx
author: jukibom
body: When people are toggling rapidly between flight modes, the sfx can be grating.

title: Add a default on or off state check for the camera rotation function
author: jukibom
body: Currently always defaults on, some folk may want to it off unless requested (e.g. in the case where an otherwise important input is being used by it on a gamepad).

title: Add a summary panel to the end screen
author: jukibom
body:
![image](https://github.com/jukibom/FlyDangerous/assets/5649179/6243f52b-3041-4921-9b92-27d8f2523b45)

"Could we get like, a pane here which shows the times for the medals, our highest achieved medal on the track, and a comparison of the last time against PB or something? Like a run recap and medals pane?

So that you don't have to go out to the menu or compare your newest time to your best time to see how you did outside of the short display upon finishing the track and the splits popups?"

title: Rework the config files
author: jukibom
body:
I am going to ABSOLUTELY HATE myself doing this but it needs to happen, and it needs a migration path from [braindead stupid land] to [glorious sensible town].

- [ ] Change prefs to struct system rather than string lookup which is most likely a bit of a CPU slap given how often they're used now
- [ ] Move graphics prefs into their own json file, excluded from steam cloud
- [ ] Move regular prefs to their own file, included in steam cloud
- [ ] Move input prefs to a folder, build out a simple profile system to allow for multiple. Store as sane human-readable json.
- [ ] Do a detection step on first run - check for instances of old prefs and if new prefs do not exist, execute a migration script (with and a tooltip popup or something). Do not delete the file on completion so old versions will continue to work.
- [ ] Celebrate the completion of this gargantuan refactor safe in the knowledge that all of the painful regressions and migration bullshit will achieve effectively nothing except people won't have to look at escaped freaking json when debugging the worst part of unity (input). 
- [ ] Consider life choices

title: `Clear All Bindings` breaks composite interactive rebind
author: jukibom
body:
Because of some funky madness in the unity input system, composite button binds (e.g. single axis button binds, like pitch up or whatever) will fail to launch an interactive rebind. 

> ArgumentException: Expecting name at position 0 in ''
> Parameter name: text
>  at UnityEngine.InputSystem.Utilities.NameAndParameters.ParseNameAndParameters (System.String text, System.Int32& index, System.Boolean nameOnly) [0x00072] in <ae71ea95a71f4bf2ab954819e7ec24bd>:0 
>  at UnityEngine.InputSystem.Utilities.NameAndParameters.Parse (System.String text) [0x00010] in <ae71ea95a71f4bf2ab954819e7ec24bd>:0 
>  at UnityEngine.InputSystem.InputBinding.GetNameOfComposite () [0x00010] in <ae71ea95a71f4bf2ab954819e7ec24bd>:0 
>  at UnityEngine.InputSystem.InputActionRebindingExtensions+RebindingOperation.WithTargetBinding (System.Int32 bindingIndex) [0x00079] in <ae71ea95a71f4bf2ab954819e7ec24bd>:0 
>  at UnityEngine.InputSystem.InputActionRebindingExtensions.PerformInteractiveRebinding (UnityEngine.InputSystem.InputAction action, System.Int32 bindingIndex) [0x000e7] in <ae71ea95a71f4bf2ab954819e7ec24bd>:0 
>  at Menus.Options.RebindAction.PerformInteractiveRebind (UnityEngine.UI.Text bindingText, UnityEngine.InputSystem.InputAction action, System.Int32 bindingIndex, Menus.Options.BindingType bindingType, System.Boolean allCompositeParts) [0x000d9] in <d0750eebd1924722810ac3e0948b3922>:0 
>  at Menus.Options.RebindAction.StartInteractivePrimaryRebind () [0x0006f] in <d0750eebd1924722810ac3e0948b3922>:0 

What fun!

title: Camera jitter when fps is low (e.g. when generating terrain)
author: jukibom
body: This happened at some point with a cinemachine package update so I suspect it's an error in the interpolation logic. Might be a fix with execution order of operation. Test with some insane resolution or a ton of ghosts and the bug should be obvious.

title: Mac terrain gen is borked again
author: jukibom
body:
No idea how or when but it's failing on Intel, M1 and M2 so ... across the board.

At this point I may just disable the native terrain gen libs for apple and throw my hands in the air but that's much slower and really sucks after it was working -.-

title: Practice Mode (Save States)
author: nova-triio
body:
Courtesy of (@)RedshiftVelocities:

"Feature idea: A checkpoint system to allow you to practice tricky spots within each map. It would save position, velocity, boost status, etc. from a previous run at your command, and then you can return to it whenever you like. Obviously it wouldn't count to scoreboard times."

--- 

A good example is how geometry dash handles practice mode, placing little mini checkpoints and allowing easy deletion and manual placing of new ones. It, like Redshift's suggestion, also saves velocity and momentum. Could also be handled as a single placeable checkpoint to resume from as opposed to lots of little ones placed while progressing. That may be the better option. 

Please expand in comments if necessary.
